data.title: OpenAI plans to appeal €15M fine by Italian data protection regulator
data.description:
• OpenAI plans to appeal a €15M, or $15.59M, fine by Garante, Italy's data protection agency, which was issued to the startup on Friday for allegedly training ChatGPT without adequate legal basis or transparency.
  • The investigation focused on a period from November 2022 through March 2023. Since then, ChatGPT has launched its Privacy Centre, where users can opt out of their data being used for artificial intelligence training.
  • "The Garante's decision is disproportionate, and we will appeal," said an OpenAI spokesperson, in a statement to Seeking Alpha. "When the Garante ordered us to stop offering ChatGPT in Italy in 2023, we worked with them to reinstate it a month later."
  • "They've since recognised our industry-leading approach to protecting privacy in AI, yet this fine is nearly twenty times the revenue we made in Italy during the relevant period," the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) company added. "We believe the Garante's approach undermines Italy's AI ambitions, but we remain committed to working with privacy authorities worldwide to offer beneficial AI that respects privacy rights."

data.title: JPMorgan spotlights 12 consumer names in its 'stocks for 2025' list
data.description:
JPMorgan's U.S. equity analysts have unveiled their "Stocks for 2025," highlighting some of their top investment ideas. These selections span growth, value, income, and short strategies, offering a diverse range of compelling ideas.

One area that was specifically highlighted was the consumer space, with the investment institution picking 12 companies in particular. Outlined below are those 12 stocks along with each of their price targets:

AerCap Holdings N.V. (AER): Price target $108.

Birkenstock Holding (BIRK): Price target $81.

BorgWarner Inc. (BWA): Price target $51.

Carvana (CVNA): Price target $300.

DraftKings (DKNG): Price target $53.

PulteGroup (PHM): Price target $153.

Post Holdings (POST): Price target $135.

Restaurant Brands International (QSR): Price target $80.

Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL): Price target $253.

SharkNinja (SN): Price target $120.

United Airlines Holdings (UAL): Price target $81.

Wayfair (W): Price target $81.

While JPMorgan didn’t specifically mention any exchange-traded funds, investors can still look to them to gain further exposure to the consumer space. Listed below are some popular consumer focused ETFs:

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI).

Consumer Staples ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS).

data.title: Xometry rises 11% as Wedbush initiates coverage with Outperform rating
data.description:
Xometry (NASDAQ:XMTR) rose 11% as Wedbush initiated coverage on the stock with an Outperform rating as it sees the firm to be well-positioned to take share within the global custom manufacturing industry.

"We see a multi-year runway of relatively high growth versus digital marketplace peers," the analysts said in a note.

The firm's high growth is expected to be driven by rising adoption among both buyers and suppliers, deepening penetration with enterprise accounts, expanding access to a larger set of manufacturing capabilities, broadening services offered to suppliers, and the prospect of continued international growth, Wedbush said.

"The company more than doubled the number of buyers and suppliers on the platform over the past three years, and we see a long runway of growth as awareness rises and initiatives to increase spending among existing buyers take hold," the analysts added.

Shares in the company were up 13.5% since the start of the year.

SA analysts rate the firm at Buy, while SA quant rates it at Strong Buy.

Wedbush has set a price target of $48.

data.title: Global auto sales to reach 89.6M in 2025—S&P Global Mobility
data.description:
Global new light vehicle sales are expected to increase 1.7% year-on-year to 89.6 million units in 2025, according to a forecast by S&P Global Mobility.

The report added the global light vehicle sales for the full year of 2024 are anticipated to touch 88.2 million units, a rise of 1.7% from the prior year, helped by ongoing inventory restocking, as supply chains have become more steady.

In the U.S., the report expects sales volumes to reach 16.2 million units in 2025, which would be an increase of 1.2% from the projected 2024 level of 16 million units.

"2025 brings with it mixed opportunities and uncertainty for the auto industry as a new administration and policy proposals take hold," said Chris Hopson, manager of North American light vehicle sales forecasting for S&P Global Mobility.

"Vehicle pricing levels are expected to decline but remain high; interest rates are expected to shift further downwards, but inflation levels are anticipated to remain sticky, and new vehicle inventory should also progress, but careful management is expected too. Combined with an uneasy consumer, we project this translates to mild growth prospects for auto sales," Hopson added.

In Western/Central European market, the report expects sales are expected to come just under 15 million units in 2024, as customers continued to exercise caution.

"Into 2025, this storyline will intensify as strict 2025 emission rules further influence the market mix and topline, S&P Global Mobility forecasts the market flatlining around 15 million units, up by just 0.1% y/y," the report said about the European market.

In mainland China, for 2024, the sales are expected to at least 25.8 million, helped by the combination of the CNY130 billion extension of New Energy Vehicle incentives, and the new CNY75 billion trade-in scheme.

The report said the global light vehicle production is set to touch 89.1 million units, a 1.6% decrease as compared to the prior year. All the regions, except mainland China and South America experienced decline.

For next year, S&P Global Mobility expects global light vehicle production levels to decline by 0.4%, to 88.7 million units.

S&P Global Mobility sees global sales for battery electric passenger vehicles to post 15.1 million units for 2025.

Potentially relevant automotive stocks: Ford (F), General Motors (GM), Stalantis (STLA), Honda (HMC), Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF), Mazda (OTCPK:MZDAF), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANF), BMW (BMWYY), and Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF).

data.title: Biden administration to blacklist chip designer that ordered TSMC chip- report
data.description:
The Biden administration is moving to blacklist chip designer Sophgo, which illegally ordered a chip used in a Huawei AI processor, Reuters reported, citing an unnamed source.

Sophgo will be placed in the U.S. Commerce Department's Entity List. It is an affiliate of bitcoin mining equipment firm Bitmain.

The company was reportedly flagged for ordering a chip from Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) that matched one found on Huawei's Ascend 910B AI processor.

data.title: CAE Inc. gains as activist takes stake, wants to help with CEO search
data.description:
CAE Inc. (NYSE:CAE) rose 3% as an activist disclosed a stake and wants to help the flight simulator find a new chief executive officer.

Browning West LP accumulated a 4.3% stake in CAE, according to a letter the activist sent to the company's board on Friday.

"We are encouraged that the Board has taken the first steps toward recruiting a new CEO," Browning West partners Usman Nabi and Peter Lee wrote in the letter. "However, we believe CAE must recruit a proven CEO with a verifiable track record of value creation. We urge the Board not to act hastily in its CEO search, but rather to engage with us to collectively recruit the best possible leader."

CAE (CAE) announced last month that president and chief executive officer, Marc Parent, will be leaving the company at next year's annual general meeting in August 2025.

data.title: House rejects Trump-backed government funding plan, risking partial shutdown (updated)
data.description:
House Speaker Mike Johnson said the chamber will vote Friday morning legislation to provide temporary funding to the government, though it's unclear what is in the package, NBC News reported. Rep. Ralph Norman (R-SC) said the Rules Committee will meet soon to advance a spending plan. (Added 10:54 AM ET)

That follows the House's failure to pass a stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump late Thursday. With 38 Republicans joining almost all the Democrats to vote against the plan, the measure failed with a vote of 174-235, falling short of garnering a simple majority, let alone the two-thirds threshold needed, the Associated Press reported.

After the vote, House Speaker Johnson said, "We're going to regroup, and we will come up with another solution, so stay tuned."

The ill-fated bill had reduced to 116 pages an earlier 1,500-page proposal by stripping out a number of add-ons, including the first pay raise for lawmakers in over a decade, that would have provided as much as a 3.8% increase.

In response to the failed vote, Trump, who derailed a bipartisan funding bill earlier this week, posted on social media, "If there is going to be a shutdown of the government, let it begin now, under the Biden Administration." (Added 10:49 AM)

Meanwhile, Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) said on the Senate floor on Friday that Republicans should bring back the agreement that was negotiated with Democrats earlier this week. (Added 10:56 AM)

The new plan had increased the debt ceiling, which wasn't a part of the initial proposal. That turned Rep. Chip Roy of Texas against the cobbled-together bill. "My position is simple — I am not going to raise or suspend the debt ceiling (racking up more debt) without significant & real spending cuts attached to it," he said, according to reporting by Bloomberg News.

House Democratic Leader Hakeem Jefferies called the Trump-backed Republican bill "laughable," adding that "it's not a serious proposal."

Last week, a bipartisan bill that would have extended funding to March 14 had come together, only to be torpedoed by Trump after Elon Musk had objected to spending that had been tacked onto the continuing resolution.

The inability to bring together Republicans to get a spending bill passed weakens Johnson's hold on the speaker role, which comes up for a vote in early January. That's reminiscent of Kevin McCarthy, who, after 15 votes, became speaker on Jan. 7, 2023, then lost it on Oct. 3, 2023, after he passed a spending bill with the help of Democrats. Mike Johnson secured the gavel on Oct. 25.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bitcoin dives under $100K as Trump-fueled surge reverses after Fed
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped to below the $100K mark on Friday, retreating from this week's record high of just above $108K, as the Federal Reserve's hawkish tone on Wednesday shifted market sentiment as the year-end approaches.

The first-ever cryptocurrency, which more than doubled in value this year, tumbled as much as 10% to $92.6K earlier in the session, before paring losses to $97.1K at 10:51 a.m. ET. Altcoins were also hit with selling pressure, with ether (ETH-USD) sliding 6.2%, dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -12%, solana (SOL-USD) -7.4%, cardano (ADA-USD) -6.4% and XRP (XRP-USD) -4.2%.

The increased volatility comes after the U.S. central bank on Wednesday cut borrowing costs again, but indicated a slower pace of rate reductions in 2025 and beyond with inflation proving sticky.

Before the Fed's hawkish signals, crypto prices were buoyed by crypto-friendly Donald Trump's victory last month in the U.S. presidential election.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell noted in his post-meeting press conference that the Fed "not allowed to hold bitcoin (BTC-USD)," and it's not looking for a law change that would allow that.

Also weighing on crypto sentiment, El Salvador said it will scale back on its bitcoin-related policies, including selling or discontinuing its bitcoin (BTC-USD) wallet Chivo, as part of a new loan deal with the International Monetary Fund.

Crypto-linked stocks, meanwhile, largely eked out intraday gains: MicroStrategy (MSTR) +8.2%, Coinbase (COIN) +1.8%, Galaxy Digital Holdings (OTCPK:BRPHF) +2.8%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) +1.1%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -1.2%, Bit Digital (BTBT) +1.6%.

data.title: State Street upgraded to Neutral at J.P. Morgan on net interest income benefits
data.description:
J.P. Morgan upgraded State Street (NYSE:STT) to Neutral from Underweight due to expected benefit to net interest income from a turnaround in deposit growth and continued growth in sponsored repos.

Among trust banks, State Street (NYSE:STT) benefits the most from the sponsored repos growth, analyst Vivek Juneja said. "These should offset continued long-term moderate growth in servicing fees and more concentrated revenue mix, which keeps us at Neutral," he said.

Overall, trust banks should benefit from continued high Treasury issuance and in net interest income from the end of quantitative tightening, Juneja said in the firm's trust bank 2025 outlook.

J.P. Morgan's Neutral view on State Street (STT) aligns with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold.

State Street (STT) stock gained 1.5% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: RxSight cut to hold at Stifel on competition concerns
data.description:
• RxSight (NASDAQ:RXST), a maker of light-adjustable intraocular lenses, traded lower on Friday after Stifel downgraded the company to Hold from Buy, citing concerns over its market competition.
  • Citing a survey of LAL-trained surgeons, analyst Thomas Stephan pointed to a worse-than-feared competition in the U.S. premium IOL market, and he projected to a potential modest weakening of LAL/LAL+ volume expectations in 2025.
  • Stephan argued that JNJ (JNJ), with its new Tecnis Odyssey lens, had gained prominence in the survey amid the imminent market entry of Alcon’s (ALC) PanOptix Pro.
  • “Several positives still exist within our checks, and long-term fundamentals remain intact—and although shares have pulled back recently, we listen to the diligence and move to the sidelines,” Stephan wrote, slashing his price target on RXST to $40 from $65.
